title: Build LLM Apps Without Code: Dify

modules.title: What Dify Is, How It Compares, and When to Use It Instead of the API
modules.sub: Understand where Dify fits and when it wins
modules.description: Learners explore what Dify is, how it differs from calling LLM APIs directly or using tools like ChatGPT and Make, and what tradeoffs come with each approach. By the end, they can make a confident, defensible decision about whether Dify is the right tool for a given use case.

modules.title: Building Your First LLM Application: Chatbots, Agents, and Workflow Automations
modules.sub: Go from blank canvas to a working LLM app
modules.description: Learners build their first functional application in Dify by choosing an app type — chatbot, agent, or workflow automation — and configuring it with prompts, model settings, and logic. They leave with a working prototype they built themselves, without writing any backend code.

modules.title: Connecting Knowledge Bases: RAG Pipelines Without Engineering Support
modules.sub: Ground your app in real internal documents and data
modules.description: Learners upload documents, configure chunking and retrieval settings, and connect a knowledge base to an existing Dify app so it can answer questions grounded in internal content. They test retrieval quality and make practical adjustments to improve accuracy — all without touching a vector database directly.

modules.title: Integrating External Tools, APIs, and Data Sources into Dify Workflows
modules.sub: Extend your app by connecting it to the outside world
modules.description: Learners add external capabilities to their Dify workflows by connecting APIs, built-in tools, and live data sources through Dify's tool integration layer. They configure authentication, map inputs and outputs, and test that their app retrieves and acts on real external information correctly.

modules.title: Deploying, Monitoring, and Iterating on Production LLM Apps in Dify
modules.sub: Ship your app and keep it working well over time
modules.description: Learners publish their Dify application to a shareable endpoint, review usage logs and conversation traces to spot failure patterns, and make targeted prompt or configuration changes based on what they observe. They finish with a deployed, monitored app and a repeatable process for improving it after launch.

synopsis: Dify is an open-source platform that lets analysts, product managers, and domain experts build and deploy production-grade LLM applications — including RAG pipelines and multi-step agents — without writing backend code. This course gives non-engineers the hands-on skills to go from idea to deployed application, including connecting internal knowledge bases and external APIs.
rationale: The bottleneck for most organizations is not access to AI models but the ability of domain experts to build useful applications from them without waiting in an engineering queue — Dify directly addresses this gap, yet adoption is constrained by a lack of practical training for non-technical professionals.
learning_outcome: Professionals can independently design, build, and deploy a functional LLM-powered application using Dify — including a connected knowledge base or external tool integration — without engineering support.
source_signals: Open source AI is the path forward (score: 5021)
nearest_existing: The Anatomy of LLM Data Exfiltration
